rxn_class_summary: Visible-light photocatalytic dearomative [2+2] cycloaddition of N-(omega-alkenoyl)indoles with alkenes via energy transfer to give cyclobutane-fused indoline polycycles

key_intermediates.name: Triplet excited indole substrate 1a-T1 (sensitized via EnT from Ir photocatalyst)
Open-shelled singlet biradical INT1-OSS (after MECP1 spin crossing)

electron_flow: Ir(III) photocatalyst is excited by visible light and transfers triplet energy to the N-acylindole substrate, generating a triplet diradical localized at indole C2/C3; this attacks the tethered alkene in a tail-to-tail fashion to give a triplet 1,4-biradical, which crosses to the open-shelled singlet surface (MECP) and closes to the cyclobutane-fused indoline product. No net SET between PC and substrate occurs (substrate redox potentials lie outside PC range).
rate_determining_step: C-C bond formation via tail-to-tail transition state TS1 (14.5 kcal/mol barrier from 1a-T1; head-to-head TS1' at 91.9 kcal/mol is disfavored by 14.9 kcal/mol)

probe_reagents.name: 2,5-dimethylhexa-2,4-diene (triplet quencher)
probe_reagents.observation: Standard reaction strongly inhibited, yield drops to 2%, supporting a triplet energy-transfer mechanism

probe_reagents.name: Trifluoroethanol (CF3CH2OH) titration monitored by 13C NMR
probe_reagents.observation: Downfield shift and line broadening of the acyl carbonyl 13C signal upon adding TFE, indicating H-bonding between solvent and substrate carbonyl that lowers triplet energy

probe_reagents.name: Stern-Volmer quenching with 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Photocatalyst emission efficiently quenched by N-(omega-alkenoyl)indole 1a

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: E(ox) = 1.70 V vs SCE; no reductive peak observed - outside redox window of Ir PC (E1/2(III*/II)=1.21 V, E1/2(III*/IV)=-0.89 V), ruling out photoinduced electron transfer

notes: Two competing cyclization modes were computed: favored tail-to-tail (TS1 77.0 kcal/mol, via INT1/INT1-OSS/TS2 to product 2a at 9.6 kcal/mol) vs disfavored head-to-head (TS1' 91.9 kcal/mol, via INT1'/INT1'-OSS/TS2'). Three undetected diastereomers (2a-A', 2a-B, 2a-B') were also located on the PES and shown to be kinetically/thermodynamically inaccessible. Mechanism involves two minimum energy crossing points (MECP1 48.4, MECP2 80.1 kcal/mol) between triplet and open-shelled singlet surfaces. H-bonding from CF3CH2OH solvent to the acyl carbonyl is proposed to lower the substrate triplet energy, enabling efficient EnT from Ir(dFCF3ppy)2(dtbbpy)PF6.
